request.question: Which band is from Ohio, Guided by Voices or Gravity Kills?

title: TVT Records
Superstarved
Guided by Voices
Gravity Kills (album)
Jim Marcus
Rocky Morton
Gravity Kills
Hypersonic Hyperphonic
Perversion (album)
Falling (Gravity Kills song)
sentences: TVT Records was an American record label founded by Steve Gottlieb.
Over the course of its 25-year history, the label released some 25 Gold, Platinum and Multi-platinum releases.

Its roster included Nine Inch Nails, Ja Rule, Lil Jon, Underworld, KMFDM, Gravity Kills, The KLF, The Baldwin Brothers, Sevendust, Nothingface, the Wellwater Conspiracy, Brian Jonestown Massacre, The Holloways, The Cinematics, Buck-O-Nine, DJ Hurricane, Speech and Pitbull.

Its biggest commercial successes were the triple platinum Nine Inch Nails's "Pretty Hate Machine", two double platinum releases by Lil Jon, and platinum releases by Snoop Dogg and Tha Eastsidaz, Dashboard Confessional, Default and Ying Yang Twins as well as gold releases by Sevendust, Gravity Kills and The Black Crowes and Jimmy Page.
Additionally, TVT also had success overseas, achieving a gold release in Germany and Sweden with The Connells and scored platinum and gold records in Canada with Default.
Superstarved (stylized as superstarved★ or superstarved*) is the third studio album by Gravity Kills, released March 19, 2002.
The album is described as "more riff-oriented" and "grindier".
Guided by Voices (often abbreviated as GBV) is an American indie rock band from Dayton, Ohio.
It has made frequent personnel changes but always maintained the presence of principal songwriter Robert Pollard.
Gravity Kills is the debut studio album by industrial rock band Gravity Kills.
It was released in 1996 on TVT Records.
The album sold just under 500,000 copies.

James Marcus (born March 22, 1966) is a musician, artist, political activist, and respected typographer who is best known for his work as the lead singer and founder of the Industrial band Die Warzau.

He is credited as engineer, remixer, vocalist, songwriter, percussionist, drummer, bass player, pianist, keyboardist or artist on albums by Die Warzau, GoFight, Sister Machine Gun, Björk, Pigface, Pansy Division, The Swans, Haloblack, Chris Connelly, KMFDM, Skrew, Machines of Loving Grace, Stabbing Westward, Gravity Kills, Everplastic, Screaming Rachel, Dessau, Chemlab, The Final Cut, Testify, Mindfunk, Little Louis and various other artists across many different genres.

His work can be heard on some of the most popular tracks by many of these groups, such as the Pigface's "Asphole", "Aboriginal", "Steamroller", etc., KMFDM's "Light", Sister Machine gun's "Wired", "Lung", "Nothing", etc.
Rocky Morton (born 1955) is an English director.
He was the co-creator of Max Headroom and co-director on "Super Mario Bros".
Various music videos by Tom Tom Club, Talking Heads, Gravity Kills, Orgy, George Harrison and Miles Davis are credited to Morton.
Videos include "New Frontier".
Alongside his partner, Annabel Jankel, the duo made their television debut with the original "," and its Americanized version.
The duo made their big-screen debut with the remake of the film noir classic, "D.O.A.," starring Dennis Quaid and Meg Ryan.
Gravity Kills is an American industrial rock band from St. Louis, Missouri.
Their music was described by one critic as "a blending of eerie industrial rock with a pop-infused melodic chorus and a bit of hard-core head banging."
Hypersonic Hyperphonic is the second studio album by Tinfed, released on December 3, 1996 by Re-Constriction Records.

"Sonic Boom" noted that "Tinfed is one of those bands who would fit perfectly on tour with the likes of God Lives Underwater, Stabbing Westward, Filter & Gravity Kills which means they should do rather well with the current crossover audience."
"Perversion" is a 1998 album by Industrial Rock act Gravity Kills.
"Falling" is a song by industrial rock band Gravity Kills from the album "Perversion", released by TVT Records in 1998.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Produce a single, concise, structured first-hop evidence report as the `summary`. The summary must do all of the following and only include content that helps determine what to fetch next:

- Enumerate concrete facts found in the provided passages. For each fact include the passage source label and either a very short quoted fragment (up to about 15 words) when a decisive short fact appears or a brief paraphrase otherwise. Keep each fact to one short line.

- Explicitly identify the bridge entity(ies) and any missing or ambiguous fact(s) that prevent answering the question from the given passages. If a name or identifier in the question corresponds to multiple candidate pages present in `passages`, list every candidate variant and mark the name as ambiguous; do not choose between them.

- Prescribe concrete, prioritized next-hop retrieval targets to resolve each missing or ambiguous element. For each target, specify which page(s) or disambiguation page to fetch and what exact section, keyword, or datum to extract (for example: specific field such as birth year, office held, list membership, dates, or the section heading to check). Prioritize targets (1, 2, 3...) when helpful.

- Only produce a final answer in `summary` if every required fact for every entity is present and unambiguous in the supplied passages; otherwise do not produce a final answer and focus on retrieval guidance.

Formatting and content constraints:
- Keep the summary concise (a short paragraph or a few short bullet-like lines).
- Produce only the single output field `summary` and nothing else.
- Preserve source-linked evidence; do not assert facts without a passage label.
- Do not invent facts or assume which ambiguous candidate is intended.
- Avoid copying long verbatim spans; use short quoted fragments when decisive and paraphrase otherwise.
- When an expected authoritative page is missing from `passages`, note that absence and include that page title as a next-hop retrieval target.

Input: fields `question` and `passages`. Output: one field `summary` meeting the requirements above.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

You are to produce exactly one compact second-hop retrieval query that targets the missing bridge or supporting evidence referenced by summary_1. Follow these operational rules when constructing the single `query` string:

- Use visible anchor tokens from `question` or `summary_1` (named works, titles, artist names, years or other identifiers only if present) and append a short retrieval cue naming the bridge sought (e.g., artist, album, recording, genre, season count, passage, supporting quote).
- Emit one concise line (one string assigned to `query`), roughly ≤ 10–12 words, formatted either as a short interrogative that names the anchors or as a compact keyword string (anchors + bridge keywords).
- If summary_1 explicitly requests a missing attribute or passage, emit the focused retrieval query rather than asking for clarification.
- Do not include answers, conclusions, enumerations, provenance notes, speculative language, or user-facing explanations; do not invent entities or add tokens that are not present in the inputs.
- Include year or other identifier tokens only if they appear in the provided inputs and help disambiguate.
- Keep wording retrieval-oriented and terse. Preserve the fallback that if retrieval using this query later fails, downstream steps may request clarification.

Output contract: produce exactly one field `query` whose value is the single compact retrieval query line described above.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Instructions for producing `summary` (single field only):
1. Merge the first-hop `context` and the second-hop `passages` into one compact integrated `summary` that preserves the primary answer name(s) and only includes passage-supported facts needed to justify or disambiguate that answer.
2. If `context` already contains a concise answer (a short sentence or a name), keep that content as the first sentence of `summary` when it is consistent with facts in `passages`. If `context` lacks a concise answer, create a concise first sentence that names the primary answer(s), using the canonical labels from `context` when available and otherwise from `passages`.
3. Expand the summary only when `passages` provide explicit, matching second-hop evidence about one or more named entities referenced in `context`. When expansion is appropriate, append at most one short supporting sentence per named entity. Each supporting sentence must begin with the entity's full name as shown in the inputs and paraphrase up to a few core attributes that are directly present in the `passages` (examples: nationality, a notable award/status, a current affiliation/team, a birth date, membership/partnership relation, or an explicit founding/formation fact).
4. Keep `summary` concise: typically 1–2 sentences for a single entity; allow up to 3 sentences only when multiple distinct named entities each require one supporting sentence. Prioritize the facts most directly necessary to answer the `question`.
5. Only assert facts that are directly supported by the provided `passages` or `context`. Do not introduce new entities, dates, awards, affiliations, roles, or causal claims that are not present in the inputs.
6. Prefer paraphrase over long verbatim copying. Use short quoted phrases only when the exact short phrase (roughly ≤10 words) appears in the `passages` and quoting preserves clarity.
7. If `context` and `passages` conflict, prefer facts that are explicitly present in the `passages`: update the concise answer to match passage-supported content rather than preserving a contradicted `context`.
8. If the retrieved `passages` do not supply explicit supporting evidence for named entities in `context`, keep the `summary` concise and do not expand or speculate.
9. Output exactly one field named `summary` whose value is the integrated post-retrieval summary text. Do not output additional fields, metadata, or commentary.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Follow these operational rules when producing `answer`:
- Use only the content of `question`, `summary_1`, and `summary_2` as your evidential basis. Do not introduce new facts or external information.
- If both `summary_1` and `summary_2` clearly and unambiguously support the same binary (yes/no) conclusion to the question, set `answer` to exactly one lowercase token: yes or no. The `answer` field must contain nothing else (no punctuation, capitalization, quotes, explanation, or additional text).
- Decide which token to emit by aggregating the visible summaries only. Treat `summary_2` as the later integrated evidence; do not let unresolved language in `summary_1` alone prevent emitting the single-token result if both summaries now unambiguously agree.
- If the summaries conflict, are ambiguous, incomplete, hedged such that the relation is unresolved, or if the question is non-binary, do not apply the single-token rule; instead follow the agent's existing final-answer fallback behavior for unresolved or non-binary cases (which may produce a fuller response or an uncertainty signal).

Produce only the `answer` field as specified.

response.hop1_query: Which band is from Ohio, Guided by Voices or Gravity Kills?
hop1_docs: Guided by Voices | Guided by Voices (often abbreviated as GBV) is an American indie rock band from Dayton, Ohio.  It has made frequent personnel changes but always maintained the presence of principal songwriter Robert Pollard.

Let's Go Eat the Factory | Let's Go Eat the Factory is the 16th album by Dayton, Ohio rock group Guided by Voices.  The album is the first since their 2004 dissolution, and the first to feature the band's classic lineup since 1996's "Under the Bushes Under the Stars".  The album was produced by the band and recorded at the homes of members Tobin Sprout, Mitch Mitchell, and Greg Demos.  As with previous albums, it features the band's famously lo-fi 4-track sound as well as more modern production.  It is also the first Guided by Voices album to be released under the Guided by Voices, Inc. label.
Sunfish Holy Breakfast | Sunfish Holy Breakfast is an EP by Guided by Voices, a band from Dayton, Ohio.  It was released on 19 November 1996.
Falling (Gravity Kills song) | "Falling" is a song by industrial rock band Gravity Kills from the album "Perversion", released by TVT Records in 1998.
Gravity Kills (album) | Gravity Kills is the debut studio album by industrial rock band Gravity Kills.  It was released in 1996 on TVT Records.  The album sold just under 500,000 copies.

Gravity Kills | Gravity Kills is an American industrial rock band from St. Louis, Missouri.  Their music was described by one critic as "a blending of eerie industrial rock with a pop-infused melodic chorus and a bit of hard-core head banging."

Andrei Găluț | Andrei Găluț (] ; born 2 August 1984) is a Romanian rock singer.  He won the 2007–08 season of Prima TV's "Megastar" He was the lead singer of metalcore band Goodbye to Gravity from 2010 until 2015 when he was seriously injured in the Colectiv nightclub fire which killed the other four members of the band.  He was the only member of Goodbye to Gravity to survive the fire.
response.summary_1:
- Fact: [1] Guided by Voices — quoted: "from Dayton, Ohio."  
- Fact: [6] Gravity Kills — quoted: "from St. Louis, Missouri."  
- Bridge entities: "Guided by Voices" and "Gravity Kills" are both present and unambiguous in the passages.  
- Missing/ambiguous facts: none; both bands' home cities are stated.  
- Final answer: Guided by Voices is from Ohio (Gravity Kills is from Missouri).
response.hop2_query: Guided by Voices Dayton Ohio and Gravity Kills St. Louis origin sources
hop2_docs: Propeller (Guided by Voices album) | Propeller is the fifth album by Dayton, Ohio indie rock group Guided by Voices.

Dayton, Ohio–19 Something and 5 | Dayton, Ohio - 19 Something and 5 is a 2000 EP by Guided by Voices.  "This is a song about smoking dope, having cookouts, and hanging out in the West Side, its called Dayton, Ohio 19 Something circa head 5", as put by front-man, Robert Pollard.

Guided by Voices | Guided by Voices (often abbreviated as GBV) is an American indie rock band from Dayton, Ohio.  It has made frequent personnel changes but always maintained the presence of principal songwriter Robert Pollard.

Motivational Jumpsuit | Motivational Jumpsuit is the 20th album by Dayton, Ohio rock group Guided by Voices.  It was released in February 2014 under their own record label "Guided by Voices Inc." It reached #18 on the Top Heatseekers chart.

Please Be Honest | Please Be Honest is the 22nd album by Dayton, Ohio rock group Guided by Voices.  It was released April 22, 2016 on Guided by Voices Inc.  Robert Pollard wrote all the music and played every instrument on the record.

Universal Truths and Cycles | Universal Truths and Cycles is the thirteenth (official) album by Dayton, Ohio indie rock group Guided by Voices.  After releasing their previous two albums on TVT Records, Guided by Voices returned to Matador Records.
English Little League | English Little League is the 19th album by Dayton, Ohio rock group Guided by Voices.
response.summary_2: Guided by Voices is from Ohio (Gravity Kills is from Missouri). Guided by Voices is an American indie rock band from Dayton, Ohio, as stated in the provided passages.
